raw_text:
Principal Full Stack Engineer

We are seeking a Principal Full Stack Engineer to lead our platform's technical evolution and define the
architectural direction for our distributed systems. This is a rare opportunity to shape not just code, but the
organization's engineering culture, technical standards, and long-term vision. You'll work across the full stack
—from our React-based control plane to our Node.js/TypeScript backend services and PostgreSQL data layer
—while mentoring senior engineers and driving high-impact technical initiatives across multiple teams. This

role reports directly to the VP of Engineering and sits at the intersection of product strategy, infrastructure, and
organizational leadership.

About the Company

Velocity Data Systems is a Series B SaaS company specializing in real-time data pipelines and analytics
infrastructure. We serve Fortune 500 companies and high-growth startups across finance, e-commerce, and

healthcare sectors. Our mission is to make enterprise-grade data infrastructure accessible and effortless to
deploy, enabling organizations to make decisions at the speed of their data.

What You'll Own

1. Define and communicate the organization-wide technical strategy, architecture patterns, and engineering

best practices; mentor Principal and Staff-level engineers to adopt and evolve these standards

2. Architect and implement complex, mission-critical full-stack systems spanning React frontends,

TypeScript/Node.js microservices, PostgreSQL databases, and AWS/GCP cloud infrastructure; serve as
the technical authority on high-risk decisions

3. Lead cross-functional technical initiatives (12+ month roadmaps) involving multiple teams; unblock
organizational bottlenecks through technical leadership, code review, and architectural guidance

4. Design and iterate on REST and GraphQL APIs that serve both internal and external consumers;

establish API design standards and security protocols across the platform

5. Drive infrastructure-as-code initiatives using AWS CDK or Terraform; establish guardrails for

deployment, monitoring (CloudWatch, Datadog), and disaster recovery across production environments

6. Conduct deep-dive code reviews and technical discussions with senior engineers; provide constructive

feedback that raises the bar for the entire engineering organization

7. Evaluate and integrate new technologies (databases, message queues, frameworks) into the stack; assess

trade-offs between scalability, maintainability, and team velocity

at Velocity Data Systems — Building the next generation of real-time data infrastructure for enterprisesSan Francisco, CA (Remote-First) • Full-Time • $280,000 - $380,000 USD 8. Build and sustain relationships with key stakeholders in Product, Design, and Operations; translate

business strategy into technical roadmap and communicate technical constraints to non-technical leaders

9. Champion a culture of technical excellence, continuous learning, and knowledge sharing; establish

communities of practice around key technologies and architectural patterns

Required

Preferred

12+ years of professional software engineering
experience, with at least 5+ years in a full-stack

Experience building real-time data systems,
streaming platforms, or analytics infrastructure

Proven background hiring, onboarding, and
developing senior engineering talent;
demonstrated mentorship of multiple Staff-
level engineers
Open-source contributions or established
thought leadership in the engineering
community (speaking, writing, recognized

expertise)

capacity building distributed systems at scale
Deep expertise in TypeScript/Node.js and at
least one frontend framework (React, Vue,
Angular); demonstrated ability to make sound
architectural decisions across the stack
Strong proficiency with SQL and relational
databases (PostgreSQL); experience designing

schemas for performance, query optimization,
and replication at scale
Proven track record defining technical strategy,
setting engineering standards, and influencing
organizational direction; prior experience as a
Staff engineer, Tech Lead, or Principal engineer
preferred
Expert-level understanding of cloud platforms
(AWS or GCP); hands-on experience with

containerization (Docker), orchestration
(Kubernetes), and infrastructure-as-code
tooling
Mastery of API design (REST, GraphQL);
understanding of authentication, authorization,
rate-limiting, and security best practices at the
API layer
Exceptional communication skills; ability to
articulate complex technical concepts to

diverse audiences (engineers, product leaders,
board members) and drive alignment on
technical decisions

Technologies

 Node.js

  TypeScript

  React

  PostgreSQL   GraphQL   REST APIs

  AWS   Docker

  Kubernetes

Terraform

Perks & Benefits

Competitive equity package (options or RSUs); participation in Series B exit upside
Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90% company contribution for employee
+ dependents
Unlimited PTO with a minimum of 20 days encouraged; separate parental leave (16 weeks for primary
caregiver, 8 weeks for secondary)

Professional development budget ($5,000/year) for conferences, courses, training; sabbatical program (4
weeks fully paid after 5 years)
Flexible work arrangements including full remote or office-based work from our SF headquarters;
monthly team gatherings and quarterly offsites in interesting locations
Wellness program including gym stipend ($150/month), mental health counseling, and meditation app
subscription
Learning stipend for books and technical certifications; internal lunch-and-learns and tech talks led by
engineering leadership

Next Steps

Apply via our careers page at velocitydatasystems.com/careers or email your resume and a brief cover letter
highlighting your most impactful architectural decisions or systems you've led to
careers@velocitydatasystems.com. Include links to your GitHub profile or portfolio work if available. We
review applications on a rolling basis and will contact qualified candidates within one week. For questions, feel
free to reach out to our Talent team at talent@velocitydatasystems.com.
nice_to_have: experience, with at least 5+ years in a full-stack
